pageDescription: Configure security device API connections so Flocks agents can call and control them directly

sidebar.heading: Rooms
sidebar.deleteHasDevices: Room "{{name}}" still has {{count}} device(s). Move or delete them first.

empty.noDevicesHint: Once you add a device, Flocks agents can call its tools
empty.roomEmptyHint: Click "Add Device" to integrate a device into this room

section.ungroupedHint: Room not found — edit the device to reassign it

wizard.title: Add Device

guide.title: Rex-assisted integration
guide.subtitle: Choose a guide or example. Rex will confirm the integration path, credential fields, and smoke-test steps to help you connect the device.

examples.supported: I want to connect a supported security device
examples.addressOnly: I only have the device URL and login method
examples.noApi: This device does not expose an API

prompts.api: I have selected API integration. Continue with API integration and guide me through confirming the vendor, product name, API docs, Base URL, authentication method, device capabilities to implement, and smoke-test steps.
prompts.browser: I have selected browser integration. Continue with browser integration and guide me through confirming the product URL, login method, target page behavior, data to collect, and automation validation steps.
prompts.addressOnly: I only have the device URL and login method. First help me determine whether an existing template can be used. If information is missing, ask only the most important question at a time, and output a device configuration JSON draft once enough details are available.
prompts.tdp: I have selected the TDP integration example. Guide me through connecting ThreatBook TDP and confirming the connection URL, API key, secret, SSL verification, and connection test steps.
prompts.onesec: I have selected the OneSEC integration example. Guide me through connecting OneSEC and confirming the Base URL, API key, secret, SSL verification, and connection test steps.

supportedList.title: Supported devices
supportedList.subtitle: Choose a vendor first, then choose the device to connect. Rex will continue guiding the integration after a device is selected.

rex.title: Rex-guided device add
rex.heading: Rex-guided integration
rex.subtitle: Describe the model, integration path, and materials you have. Rex will help confirm the template, credential fields, and test steps.
rex.welcome:
Tell me the device vendor, model, version, and any API docs, console URL, or authentication details you already have.

If the device is already supported, I will organize the values needed for the device configuration form. Once there is enough information, I will output a ```json configuration draft that the page can detect and use to fill the form. If there is no matching template yet, I will guide you through creating a custom device integration.
rex.placeholder: Describe the device, URL, authentication method, or upload related materials
rex.extractEmpty: No Rex output is available to extract yet
rex.extractNoTemplate: Could not match a device template from Rex output
rex.extractFailed: Extraction failed. Ask Rex to output a ```json device configuration draft and try again.
rex.installFirst: This template is not installed yet. Install it from FlockHub first.
rex.detectedInstall: Detected a device template, but it is not installed yet

existing.title: Existing template
existing.desc: Organize configuration fields for supported devices
existing.prompt: I want to integrate a security device that is already supported by a template. Guide me through confirming the vendor, model, connection URL, credential fields, and connection test steps.

api.title: Custom API
api.desc: Create a custom device plugin from API docs
api.prompt: I have selected API integration. Help me confirm the API docs, authentication method, device details, and steps for generating a device plugin.

webcli.title: Browser integration
webcli.desc: Create integration capability from a web console
webcli.prompt: I have selected browser integration. Guide me through preparing the product URL, login method, target pages/actions, and browser integration steps.

wizard.chooseVendorOrCustom: Choose a vendor or create a custom device integration
wizard.chooseCustomMode: Choose how this custom device should connect

api.title: API Integration
api.desc: Use this when the device exposes APIs. Provide API docs and generate a reusable device plugin.

webcli.title: Browser Integration
webcli.desc: Use this when the device has no open API. First generate and integrate the skill or CLI asset; for security devices, also generate a device plugin for the device page.

workflow.title: Workflow Integration
workflow.desc: Use this for Syslog, Kafka, or Webhook sources. The device page will guide you to the workflow integration screen for the final setup.

wizard.productHint: {{count}} product(s) — the same type can be added multiple times

config.secretConfigured: Already set · Leave unchanged to keep it, clear to remove
config.sslHint: Disable to reach internal devices with self-signed certificates
config.enabledHint: When disabled, agents will not call tools from this device

subtitle.api: Provide API documentation and generate a reusable device plugin
subtitle.webcli: Provide the product URL and target interfaces to generate a browser-integration device plugin for the device page
subtitle.workflow: Configure Syslog, Kafka, and Webhook entries on the workflow Publish page

welcome.api: Please add an API docs URL or upload the docs file directly. I will use the tool-builder skill to generate a device plugin that can be used from the device page.
welcome.webcli: Please add the product URL, target interfaces, and auth hints. I will use the web2cli skill to build and integrate the CLI or skill assets first, and then generate a device plugin when needed.
welcome.workflow: Workflow entries are not created here. Go to the workflow Publish page and configure Syslog, Kafka, or Webhook as needed.

validation.deviceNameRequired: Please enter the device product name
validation.targetInterfacesRequired: Please describe the interfaces or page behaviors to capture

toast.submitSuccess: Submitted to Rex. Continue the plugin generation in chat.

details.prepareTitle: Prepare the integration details before sending them to Rex
details.prepareIntro: After submission, you will enter the Rex conversation directly.
details.apiNext: You can continue to add API doc links, upload documentation files, or explain interface details. Once the plugin is generated, return to the device page to finish the rest of the setup.
details.webcliNext: You can continue to add page actions, capture targets, and authentication details. The final result should be a browser-integration device plugin that the device page can recognize; a CLI can remain as an optional debugging entry.

rex.apiHint: Upload API docs or describe the endpoints. Rex will clarify key gaps before generating the device plugin.
rex.webcliHint: Provide the product URL and target pages/actions. Rex will clarify login, permissions, and risky operations before generating assets.
rex.placeholder: Continue adding interface details, authentication information, or debugging notes

workflow.heading: Workflow integration currently supports Syslog, Kafka, and Webhook
workflow.body: On the workflow detail page, open the Integration tab, choose Syslog, Kafka, or Webhook for your scenario, and then push data from the device or upstream system into the matching entry point.
workflow.requirement1: Choose the integration type based on the data source: Syslog for log forwarding, Kafka for message queues, and Webhook for HTTP callbacks.
workflow.requirement2: Make sure the workflow listener address, topic, or webhook URL is reachable from the device or upstream system, and map the input fields correctly.
workflow.requirement3: After configuration, check the workflow execution history to confirm that the input data has been received and consumed.

api.docsUrlHint: A public URL is optional. After submission, you can keep uploading API docs inside the Rex conversation.
api.capabilitiesPlaceholder: All APIs by default, or limit to specific APIs
api.capabilitiesHint: All APIs are used by default. If you only need specific interfaces, describe them here.

webcli.targetInterfacesLabel: Interfaces or page behaviors to capture
webcli.targetInterfacesPlaceholder: e.g. alert list API, asset detail API, and the request triggered by the "Block IP" button
webcli.authHintPlaceholder: e.g. admin role required; request depends on Cookie + X-CSRF-Token
